user_query:
I maintain local text notes about political developments in India’s neighboring countries. Please build a small event-triggered automation that processes new update files and prepares a weekly summary plus a draft email for my discussion group.

Inputs are already in the workspace:
- input/daily_updates/ (contains *.txt daily update files; one may have the prefix ALERT_ to indicate urgent items)
- input/config/neighbors.yaml (countries of interest and aliases)
- input/recipients.yaml (email recipients)
- input/scripts/validate_updates.py (a simple validator you must run and capture output from)

Requirements:
1) Implement a command-line script or small program that can be run repeatedly and does both of the following:
   - Scan mode: On each run, detect and parse only new or changed .txt files in input/daily_updates/ since the previous run (use a state file at output/state.json to avoid reprocessing identical files; store a hash per file and the last report range).
   - Watch mode: If invoked with a flag like --watch, keep running and react when a new .txt appears in input/daily_updates/ by immediately updating outputs as in scan mode. The initial startup should also process any unprocessed files present.

2) Parsing rules for updates files (.txt):
   - Each content line represents one update and should be in the form: YYYY-MM-DD | Country | Headline | Source.
   - Ignore blank lines and lines starting with #.
   - Normalize and keep only updates whose Country matches the neighbors listed in input/config/neighbors.yaml (apply aliases if provided).
   - Treat malformed lines (e.g., missing fields) as skipped but count them for diagnostics and logging.

3) Reporting window:
   - Determine the report’s 7-day window using the maximum date among all valid updates seen so far on this run. The window is [max_date - 6 days, max_date], inclusive.
   - Only include valid updates within this window in the outputs below.

4) Outputs to create/update on each run:
   - output/weekly_summary.md: A concise weekly report with sections per country (only if that country has at least one valid update in the 7-day window). Under each country, list lines in the form "- YYYY-MM-DD: Headline (Source)" sorted by date ascending.
     • At the top, include a one-line header like "Neighbors Weekly Political Round-up (YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD)" using the computed window.
     • At the end, add a Diagnostics section that includes a verbatim copy of the stdout/stderr produced by running the validator command: python3 input/scripts/validate_updates.py input/daily_updates. This command must be executed by your automation, and its output included verbatim in the report so I can see any warnings about malformed lines.
   - output/draft_email.txt: A plain-text email draft using recipients in input/recipients.yaml with:
     • To and Cc headers listing emails from that file.
     • Subject: "Weekly neighbors round-up (YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD)" using the computed window.
     • Body: A short status summary paragraph that states the total number of valid updates and the number of countries covered in the window, followed by a breakdown with one line per country as "Country: N updates". If any processed file name starts with ALERT_, include an "Urgent alerts" subsection listing those alert items (date, country, headline) within the window. Conclude by referencing the report path output/weekly_summary.md.
   - output/automation.log: Append one line per processed file in the form "ISO8601 timestamp | processed <relative_file_path> | valid=<n_valid> skipped=<n_skipped>".
   - output/state.json: A JSON state file containing at least: a map of file paths to their content hash (e.g., sha256) and last_processed time, and the last computed report window.

5) Additional constraints:
   - Do not send real email or require Internet access; only produce the artifacts above.
   - The validator step (python3 input/scripts/validate_updates.py input/daily_updates) is mandatory each time outputs are refreshed; capture and embed its output in the Diagnostics section of output/weekly_summary.md.
   - If the validator reports warnings for malformed lines, ensure those lines are excluded from counts in the report and email but included in skipped counts in output/automation.log.

Deliverables to verify:
- output/weekly_summary.md reflecting only valid updates for neighbors, within the computed 7-day window, and including the verbatim validator output at the end.
- output/draft_email.txt as described.
- output/automation.log with an entry for each processed file.
- output/state.json tracking processed files and last report window.

You may choose the implementation language, but please ensure the script runs locally, respects the input files above, and produces exactly the outputs in the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
